author: jñānadāsa
verses: śrīdāma bale ogo rāṇī, vidāya de tora nīlamaṇi laye jāba goṣṭha vihāre godhana cāraṇa kari, āmi diba tomāra hari nivedana kari joḍa kare rāṇī bale ki balili, nā pāṭhāva vanamālī tomārā sabāo jāo vane baḍa ha’le lālane, la’ye jeo kānane pāṭhāiba tomā sabā sane śunare śrīdāma bhāi, āmāra jāoyā ha’la nāi mā vidāya nāhi dila more jñāna⋅dāsa kahe śuna, jaśodāra jīvana jāni ki nā jāni vidāya kare
en_translation:
Śrīdāma says: "O Queen! Bid farewell to your Blue Jewel. I will take Him for pastimes in the pasture. Grazing the cows, I will give (back) your Hari. I make this request with folded hands."

The Queen says: "What did you say? I will not send Vanamālī. You all go to the forest too. When He grows up, take Him to the forest with affection. I will send Him with all of you."

"Listen, O brother Śrīdāma! My going has not happened. Mother did not give me farewell." Jñāna Dāsa says: "Listen, I know Yaśodā's life; I do not know if she will bid farewell or not."
